patches.find: In 12 of 14 cases, both models predict the same observable — making them non-discriminating.
patches.replace: In 13 of 14 cases, both models predict the same observable — making them non-discriminating.
patches.rationale: SEC-4.1 Group A table shows 13 YES rows (only row #8 Telluric Cutoff is not YES). Prose says 12 but table says 13. Update to match data.

patches.find: imagine measuring your height with the same ruler in 30 rooms. You get "6 feet" every time. That is not 30 independent confirmations — it is one ruler used 30 times.
patches.replace: imagine measuring your height with the same ruler in 14 rooms. You get "6 feet" every time. That is not 14 independent confirmations — it is one ruler used 14 times.
patches.rationale: The analogy says 30 rooms/times but the actual count of shared-constant domains is 14. Update to match the data cited two sentences later.

patches.find: Its apparent altitude varies with atmospheric refraction (~0.5° near the horizon, ~0.1° at high elevation).
patches.replace: Its apparent altitude varies with atmospheric refraction (~0.5° near the horizon, ~0.02–0.03° at typical Polaris elevations of 35–60°).
patches.rationale: Standard atmospheric refraction at 35-60 degrees elevation is approximately 0.01-0.03 degrees, not 0.1 degrees. WIN-065 correctly states 0.02-0.03 degrees. Harmonize to avoid credibility damage from inflated refraction term.
Minor wording: the globe does not 'predict' zero coupling; standard physics assumes zero aetheric coupling. 'Standard physics: zero' is clearer. Curmudgeon Cycle 2 flag for SEC-4.1.
